summary:
To determine if medication 1 reduces cardiac death more than medication 2, compute the Number Needed to Treat (NNT). Using event rates—group A at 134/1500=0.0893 and group B at 210/1500=0.14—the Absolute Risk Reduction (ARR)=0.0507; thus, NNT=1/0.0507≈**20**. This result reflects a moderate improvement, requiring treatment of approximately every patient to prevent one additional death due to heart disease during follow-up.

The agent correctly calculates the Number Needed to Treat (NNT) by deriving the absolute risk reduction from observed event rates in both groups, resulting in an NNT of approximately 20. This represents a clinically interpretable yet modest benefit, indicating that around 20 patients would need to receive medication 1 instead of medication 2 to prevent one cardiac death over time.  

Final Answer: \boxed{20}  
--- 

End of Agent Reasoning---

Note: Do not include any commentary beyond the summary. Only present the final answer as requested.

\boxed{20}
